title: Characterizing bulk properties of gapped phases by smeared boundary conformal field theories: Role of duality in unusual ordering
abstract: We study the classification of the gapped phases or massive renormalization group (RG) flows dual to the massless RG flows under changing the sign of the coupling constants. Whereas our formalism is based on combining Higgs- or Nambu-Goldstone-type arguments with Cardy's smeared boundary conformal field theories (SBCFTs), several puzzling structures arise. More specifically, the established Higgs or Nambu-Goldstone type arguments on the duality imply that the natural basis for the gapped states should be constructed from a set of smeared Ishibashi states, which are unphysical in boundary critical phenomena. Hence, the module of the gapped phases can be outside of that of boundary critical phenomena, whereas one can still calculate characterizing quantities by applying SBCFTs to the models. For example, we demonstrate that the massive RG flow dual to the massless RG flow from the tricritical Ising model to the Ising model, one of the simplest massless RG flows, has this unusual structure. This can be regarded as a quantum field-theoretic analogue of order-disorder coexistence in lattice models. More generally, the resultant gapped phases usually spontaneously break non-group-like symmetry (or noninvertible symmetry). Our work provides systematic quantum field theoretic descriptions of such unusual phases with spontaneous symmetry breaking of non-group-like (or noninvertible) symmetries.
summary: This paper explores how gapped phases can exhibit unusual structures that do not belong to the standard set of boundary critical phenomena. By studying dualities in RG flows, the authors show that these phases can involve the spontaneous breaking of non-invertible symmetries, providing a new framework for describing complex phase transitions.
main_problem: The paper investigates the classification of gapped phases and massive renormalization group (RG) flows that are dual to massless RG flows under coupling constant sign changes, specifically focusing on cases where the gapped phase structure is unusual.
main_result: The authors find that the module of gapped phases can lie outside the module of boundary critical phenomena, characterized by the spontaneous breaking of non-group-like (noninvertible) symmetries and the use of unphysical smeared Ishibashi states.
method: The study utilizes a formalism combining Higgs- or Nambu-Goldstone-type arguments with Cardy's smeared boundary conformal field theories (SBCFTs).
model_or_system: The study focuses on renormalization group flows, specifically examining the massive RG flow dual to the flow from the tricritical Ising model to the Ising model.
key_observables: Characterizing quantities of gapped phases and the structure of the module of gapped states.
important_parameters: The sign of the coupling constants and the presence of non-group-like or noninvertible symmetries.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the validity of the duality between massless and massive RG flows under coupling sign changes and the applicability of SBCFTs to these models.
paper_structure: The paper introduces a formalism for classifying gapped phases using SBCFTs and Higgs-type arguments, identifies the emergence of unusual structures where gapped states are constructed from unphysical Ishibashi states, provides a specific example using the tricritical Ising model, and generalizes the findings to the breaking of non-invertible symmetries.
